parameters.jsCode: const brandProfile = $json.content[0].text; const slug = $('Build Brand Profile Request').item.json.slug; const imageUrls = $('Build Brand Profile Request').item.json.image_urls || []; const imageSection = imageUrls.length > 0 ? `\n\n== EXISTING SITE IMAGES ==\n\nThe following image URLs were scraped from the business's existing site. Use them opportunistically where they genuinely improve the design — never force them.\n\nGood uses:\n- Hero background: CSS background-image with a dark gradient overlay so text stays readable\n- About section: team photo, shop/location photo as CSS background-image with a fallback color\n- Service section: a photo clearly matching a specific service as CSS background-image with a fallback color\n\nSkip any image that looks like a third-party logo, social media icon, map embed, or ad pixel. When in doubt, skip it. If you use an image as a background, always apply a semi-transparent dark overlay.\n\nAvailable images:\n${imageUrls.map(u => '- ' + u).join('\n')}` : ''; const prompt = `You are an expert web designer creating a premium, production-grade website redesign demo for a local business. This will be shown to the business owner to win them as a client — quality and distinctiveness are critical. You have a brand profile. Use it as your actual design brief. == STEP 1: COMMIT TO A DIRECTION == Read the brand profile carefully. Before writing any code, decide: 1. THE ONE THING: What will make this design memorable and specific to this business? Not just a color — a feeling, an approach, a structural choice that fits this business and no other. 2. AESTHETIC COMMITMENT: Based on the redesign_direction and tone in the brand profile, commit to one of these directions (or define your own that fits better): - luxury/refined: editorial layouts, generous white space, elegant serifs, jewel tone accents - warm/artisanal: organic warmth, amber/earth tones, characterful serif + humanist sans - bold/energetic: high contrast, vivid accent, strong type scale, dynamic section treatments - clean/modern: tight grid, cool palette, geometric details, precise spacing - playful/friendly: rounded forms, warm expressive colors, display type with personality, motion - professional/trustworthy: structured grid, credential-forward, cool anchors, restrained palette 3. SECTION PLAN: Choose 6-8 sections this specific business needs. Not every business needs every section. ALWAYS INCLUDE: - Sticky nav - Hero (min-height 90vh) - Contact/Booking CTA - Footer INCLUDE BASED ON BUSINESS TYPE: - Marquee strip — good for multi-service businesses and location callouts - Why/Benefits (3 columns) — for trust-building services with clear differentiators - Services & Pricing — if services array has 3+ distinct offerings worth showcasing - Gallery/Environment — for businesses where physical space or work quality matters (pet grooming, salons, studios, restaurants) - Service Area — for mobile businesses or multi-location operations - About/Team — if key_differentiators are credential or people-focused - Testimonials — placeholder blocks for high-trust service businesses - Process/How It Works — for services that feel mysterious or complex to buyers DO NOT add sections that do not fit. A mobile groomer does not need a gallery of a physical location. A single-service business does not need a pricing comparison table. == STEP 2: DESIGN SYSTEM == FONTS: Choose a pairing that matches your committed direction. - luxury/editorial: Cormorant Garamond + Jost, Bodoni Moda + DM Sans, Playfair Display + DM Sans - warm/artisanal: Fraunces + Nunito, Lora + Lato, Libre Baskerville + Source Sans 3 - bold/energetic: Bebas Neue + Nunito Sans, Oswald + Open Sans, Syne + Manrope - clean/modern: DM Serif Display + Plus Jakarta Sans, Instrument Serif + Inter - playful/friendly: Lilita One + Nunito, Righteous + Quicksand, Paytone One + DM Sans - professional: Merriweather + Outfit, Libre Baskerville + Outfit NEVER use Inter, Roboto, Arial, or system-ui as the display font. COLOR PALETTE: Use the colors object from the brand profile as your starting point. Build a 4-value system: - dark: deep anchor for nav and dark sections - accent: the standout vivid color — use brand primary or accent, make it distinctive - bg: warm or cool off-white (NOT pure #ffffff) - bg-alt: slightly darker for alternating sections == STEP 3: SPATIAL COMPOSITION == Push beyond the default. Use the brand personality to drive layout: - At least ONE section must use a dramatic treatment: full-bleed background, asymmetric layout, overlapping elements, or diagonal flow - Vary section density — some spacious, some dense - Hero: avoid plain centered text on dark background unless it is exactly right. Consider left-aligned editorial, split layouts, full-bleed imagery with overlay, or oversized type as the visual anchor - Add depth to backgrounds: subtle gradient meshes, geometric shapes, layered transparencies — not flat solid colors == STEP 4: CORE CSS PATTERNS == Include these in your stylesheet, substituting your actual accent color: .rule-accent { height:1px; background:linear-gradient(90deg,transparent,[accent] 30%,[accent] 70%,transparent); } .rule-short { display:inline-block; width:48px; height:1px; background:[accent]; flex-shrink:0; } .lift { transition:transform 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1),box-shadow 0.35s ease; } .lift:hover { transform:translateY(-7px); box-shadow:0 28px 60px rgba(0,0,0,0.13); } @keyframes marquee { from{transform:translateX(0)} to{transform:translateX(-50%)} } .marquee-inner { display:flex; animation:marquee 28s linear infinite; white-space:nowrap; } .marquee-inner:hover { animation-play-state:paused; } @keyframes fadeUp { from{opacity:0;transform:translateY(22px)} to{opacity:1;transform:translateY(0)} } .fu { opacity:0; animation:fadeUp 0.7s ease both; } .d1{animation-delay:0.05s} .d2{animation-delay:0.2s} .d3{animation-delay:0.35s} .d4{animation-delay:0.5s} .d5{animation-delay:0.65s} .nav-lnk { position:relative; font-size:0.75rem; font-weight:500; letter-spacing:0.1em; text-transform:uppercase; } .nav-lnk::after { content:''; position:absolute; bottom:-2px; left:0; width:0; height:1px; background:[accent]; transition:width 0.3s; } .nav-lnk:hover::after { width:100%; } .prow { border-bottom:1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.08); } .prow:last-child { border-bottom:none; } .prow-dark { border-bottom:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); } .prow-dark:last-child { border-bottom:none; } .incl { display:flex; align-items:flex-start; gap:8px; font-size:0.8125rem; padding:5px 0; border-bottom:1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.06); } .incl:last-child { border-bottom:none; } .incl::before { content:'—'; color:[accent]; flex-shrink:0; margin-top:1px; font-size:0.75rem; } #mob-nav { display:none; } #mob-nav.open { display:flex; } ANTI-PATTERNS — never do these: - No 2x2 or 4x4 emoji badge grids anywhere - No gradient hero backgrounds (solid dark + subtle radial overlay max) - No Inter, Roboto, Arial, or system-ui as display font - No border-radius:9999px on everything - No generic purple/pink/blue gradients - No pure #ffffff backgrounds - No all-centered layouts — vary alignment meaningfully - No tags for press logos, publication badges, or award images — these are hotlink-protected and will appear broken. Render press/award sections as styled text only: publication name in uppercase with letter-spacing and muted color, separated by dividers or dots REQUIREMENTS: - Tailwind CSS via CDN: https://cdn.tailwindcss.com - Google Fonts via tags in before Tailwind - All real images from the existing site must be used as CSS background-image only, never as tags — set a fallback background-color on the same element so if the image is hotlink-blocked, the section still looks intentional - in - Mobile hamburger menu functional via JS onclick classList.toggle - All forms must be non-functional: add disabled attribute to all inputs, selects, and buttons inside forms, and include a visible banner inside the form that reads 'This is a redesign demo — form submissions are disabled.' Style the banner in a small, muted italic note so it is clear but not disruptive - Footer: "Redesign demo by derekjensen.io" - Output ONLY raw HTML starting with — no markdown, no explanation, no code fences Brand profile: ${brandProfile}${imageSection}`; const requestBody = JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6', max_tokens: 32000, messages: [{ role: 'user', content: prompt }] }); return [{ json: { requestBody, slug } }];
